questions.explanation: The dot product returns a scalar equal to ||a||·||b||·cos(theta), capturing alignment/similarity and projection.

options.A: A scalar measuring similarity/projection between the vectors
options.B: A vector perpendicular to both vectors
options.C: A matrix whose determinant equals the angle between vectors
options.D: A probability distribution over vector components

questions.question: In linear algebra, what does the dot product of two vectors primarily compute?

questions.explanation: Lists can be modified in place (append, assignment), while tuples cannot be changed after creation.

options.A: Lists are mutable; tuples are immutable
options.B: Lists can store numbers; tuples cannot
options.C: Tuples are slower to iterate than lists by definition
options.D: Lists preserve order; tuples do not

questions.question: In Python, what is the main difference between a list and a tuple?

questions.explanation: F1-score balances precision and recall, making it more informative than accuracy under class imbalance.
questions.question: Which metric is most appropriate for evaluating a binary classifier on a highly imbalanced dataset when you care about performance on the minority class?

questions.explanation: Without non-linear activations, stacked layers collapse into an equivalent linear transformation.

options.A: To introduce non-linearity so the network can model complex functions
options.B: To ensure weights always sum to 1
options.C: To make gradients exactly constant across layers
options.D: To convert a regression problem into classification

questions.question: In deep learning, what is the primary purpose of an activation function like ReLU?

questions.explanation: Gradient descent minimizes the loss by stepping in the negative gradient direction: w ← w − η∇L(w).
questions.question: You are training logistic regression with gradient descent. Which update rule is correct for parameters w with learning rate η and gradient ∇L(w)?

questions.explanation: model.eval() sets inference behavior for certain layers, and torch.no_grad() disables autograd tracking to save memory/compute.
questions.question: In PyTorch, which snippet correctly prevents gradient tracking during evaluation/inference?

questions.explanation: Nested CV separates hyperparameter selection from performance estimation, reducing optimistic bias from test leakage.

options.A: Train on all data and report training loss
options.B: Use the test set for hyperparameter tuning and report test performance
options.C: Use nested cross-validation (inner loop for tuning, outer loop for evaluation)
options.D: Shuffle labels to remove bias, then evaluate once

questions.question: You want to estimate a model’s generalization performance while tuning hyperparameters without leaking test information. Which approach is best?

questions.explanation: Gradient checkpointing trades extra compute for lower memory by not storing all activations and recomputing them during backward pass.

options.D: Remove the tokenizer and feed raw text

questions.question: You are fine-tuning a large Transformer for NLP with limited GPU memory. Which change most directly reduces activation memory during backprop while keeping the same model architecture?

questions.explanation: The deadly triad (function approximation + bootstrapping + off-policy learning) can lead to divergence due to moving targets and distribution shift.

options.A: Because Q-learning requires on-policy data and cannot use replay buffers
options.B: Because the combination of function approximation, bootstrapping, and off-policy updates can create unstable feedback loops (the 'deadly triad')
options.C: Because the Bellman equation is only valid for tabular methods

questions.question: In reinforcement learning, why can off-policy Q-learning diverge when combined with function approximation and bootstrapping (e.g., neural networks)?

questions.explanation: Mixed precision reduces gradient tensor size (and often enables faster kernels), decreasing all-reduce communication time and improving scaling.

options.A: Increase the number of DataLoader workers only
options.B: Use mixed precision (FP16/BF16) with gradient scaling to reduce communication volume
options.C: Turn off shuffling in the dataset
options.D: Switch the activation function from ReLU to tanh

questions.question: You are running distributed data-parallel training across 8 GPUs and observe poor scaling. Profiling shows long stalls during gradient synchronization. Which change most directly targets this bottleneck?
